results.evidence: 7.1 SUICIDE: If the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid without interest. No death benefit is payable.
results.llm_answer: The insurer's liability is limited to returning the premiums paid without interest; the death benefit of $300,000 is not payable if the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of the issue date.

results.question: What happens to the ROP benefit if the policy owner converts to permanent insurance?
results.expected_answer: The ROP benefit is forfeited and premiums paid to date are not returned.
results.evidence: 5.4 Upon conversion: ROP benefit is forfeited / Premiums paid to date are NOT returned
results.llm_answer: The ROP benefit is forfeited if the policy owner converts to permanent insurance.

results.question: If a premium is paid late during the grace period, how is the ROP benefit affected?
results.expected_answer: The ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment.
results.evidence: 4.5 Missed premiums cannot be made up. If a premium is paid late (during grace period), the ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment.
results.llm_answer: The ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment made during the grace period.

results.expected_answer: No. The full $32,400 end-of-term ROP requires all premiums to be paid on time. A late payment reduces the ROP benefit by 10%, so the return would be reduced.
results.evidence: 4.3 CRITICAL: To receive the full return of premium at end of term, ALL premiums must be paid on time. / 4.5 If a premium is paid late (during grace period), the ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment.
results.llm_answer: No, the full $32,400 will not be returned because a late payment reduces the ROP benefit by 10%.

results.question: If the policy owner converts to permanent insurance after 10 years of paying premiums, do they receive any refund of the premiums paid?
results.expected_answer: No. Upon conversion the ROP benefit is forfeited and premiums paid to date are not returned. The conversion privilege requires giving up the ROP benefit entirely.
results.evidence: 5.4 Upon conversion: ROP benefit is forfeited / Premiums paid to date are NOT returned
results.llm_answer: No, the policy owner does not receive any refund of the premiums paid if they convert to permanent insurance, as the ROP benefit is forfeited upon conversion.

    Years 11-15: 60% of premiums paid returned
    Years 16-19: 90% of premiums paid returned

    • Full death benefit ($300,000), PLUS
    • A proportional premium refund based on the early surrender 
      schedule above

    $300,000 death benefit + (30% × $12,960 premiums paid) = 
    $300,000 + $3,888 = $303,888

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3.1 END OF TERM ROP:
    If the insured is alive on September 15, 2044, and all 
    premiums have been paid, we will pay the policy owner 
    $32,400 (100% of premiums paid over 20 years).

3.2 EARLY SURRENDER ROP:
    If you surrender this policy before the end of term:
    
    Years 1-5:   0% premium return
    Years 6-10:  30% of premiums paid returned
    Years 11-15: 60% of premiums paid returned
    Years 16-19: 90% of premiums paid returned
    Year 20:     100% of premiums paid returned

3.3 DEATH DURING TERM:
    If death occurs during the term, beneficiaries receive:
    • Full death benefit ($300,000), PLUS
    • A proportional premium refund based on the early surrender 
      schedule above

    Example: If death occurs in Year 8, beneficiaries receive:
    $300,000 death benefit + (30% × $12,960 premiums paid) = 
    $300,000 + $3,888 = $303,888

3.4 ROP payments are made within 60 days of the applicable date.

════════════════════════════════════════

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
7.1 SUICIDE: If the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of 
    the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid without 
    interest. No death benefit is payable.

7.2 MATERIAL MISREPRESENTATION: We may contest the policy within 
    2 years of issue if there was material misrepresentation in 
    the application.

7.3 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, travel, or hazardous 
    activity exclusions apply.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SECTION 8: REINSTATEMENT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
8.1 If the policy lapses, you may apply for reinstatement within 
    5 years if:
    • You provide satisfactory evidence of insurability
    • You pay all past due premiums with interest at 6% per annum
    • The insured is still alive

8.2 Upon reinstatement, the ROP benefit is recalculated to exclude 
    the lapsed period.

8.3 A new 2-year suicide and contestability period begins upon 
    reinstatement.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SECTION 9: IMPORTANT POLICY NOTES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
9.1 This policy costs more than standard term life insurance 
    because of the return of premium feature.

9.2 The ROP benefit is NOT considered a taxable gain when paid 
    (consult your tax advisor).

9.3 This policy does NOT build cash value that can be borrowed 
    against during the term.

9.4 MISSTATEMENT OF AGE: If age was misstated, we will adjust 
    the death benefit to what the premium would have purchased 
    at the correct age. ROP amount is not affected.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SECTION 6: POLICY LOANS AND WITHDRAWALS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
6.1 Policy loans are NOT available under this policy type.

6.2 Partial withdrawals are NOT permitted.

6.3 The only way to access cash value is through:
    • Full surrender (with applicable ROP percentage), OR
    • Waiting until end of term for full ROP payment

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
7.1 SUICIDE: If the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of 
    the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid without 
    interest. No death benefit is payable.

7.2 MATERIAL MISREPRESENTATION: We may contest the policy within 
    2 years of issue if there was material misrepresentation in 
    the application.

7.3 NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, travel, or hazardous 
    activity exclusions apply.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SECTION 8: REINSTATEMENT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
8.1 If the policy lapses, you may apply for reinstatement within 
    5 years if:
    • You provide satisfactory evidence of insurability
    • You pay all past due premiums with interest at 6% per annum
    • The insured is still alive

8.2 Upon reinstatement, the ROP benefit is recalculated to exclude 
    the lapsed period.

8.3 A new 2-year suicide and contestability period begins upon 
    reinstatement.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SECTION 9: IMPORTANT POLICY NOTES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
9.1 This policy costs more than standard term life insurance 
    because of the return of premium feature.

9.2 The ROP benefit is NOT considered a taxable gain when paid 
    (consult your tax advisor).

9.3 This policy does NOT build cash value that can be borrowed 
    against during the term.

9.4 MISSTATEMENT OF AGE: If age was misstated, we will adjust 
    the death benefit to what the premium would have purchased 
    at the correct age. ROP amount is not affected.
